title: Assertion 'path_is_absolute(p)' failed at src/basic/chase.c:648, function chase().
body:
Starting with release v1.39 many systemd commands fail with:
`Assertion 'path_is_absolute(p)' failed at src/basic/chase.c:648, function chase().`

example command:
`buildah run builder sh -c /usr/bin/systemd-sysusers`

After more testing it seems unrelated to systemd version, but does only happen with buildah v1.39

This seems to be related: https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/36242

comments.body:
Same here, building an image in GitLab Pipeline with `quay.io/containers/buildah:v1.39.0` and newer fails:

```bash
Selecting previously unselected package libsystemd-shared:amd64.
Preparing to unpack .../5-libsystemd-shared_255.4-1ubuntu8.5_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking libsystemd-shared:amd64 (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Preparing to unpack .../6-libsystemd0_255.4-1ubuntu8.5_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking libsystemd0:amd64 (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) over (255.4-1ubuntu8.4) ...
Setting up libsystemd0:amd64 (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Selecting previously unselected package systemd-dev.
(Reading database ... 5776 files and directories currently installed.)
Preparing to unpack .../systemd-dev_255.4-1ubuntu8.5_all.deb ...
Unpacking systemd-dev (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Selecting previously unselected package systemd.
Preparing to unpack .../systemd_255.4-1ubuntu8.5_amd64.deb ...
Unpacking systemd (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Setting up libapparmor1:amd64 (4.0.1really4.0.1-0ubuntu0.24.04.3) ...
Setting up libargon2-1:amd64 (0~20190702+dfsg-4build1) ...
Setting up libdevmapper1.02.1:amd64 (2:1.02.185-3ubuntu3.2) ...
Setting up libjson-c5:amd64 (0.17-1build1) ...
Setting up libcryptsetup12:amd64 (2:2.7.0-1ubuntu4.2) ...
Setting up libfdisk1:amd64 (2.39.3-9ubuntu6.2) ...
Setting up libkmod2:amd64 (31+20240202-2ubuntu7.1) ...
Setting up libsystemd-shared:amd64 (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Setting up systemd-dev (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Setting up systemd (255.4-1ubuntu8.5) ...
Created symlink /etc/systemd/system/getty.target.wants/getty@tty1.service → /usr/lib/systemd/system/getty@.service.
Created symlink /etc/systemd/system/multi-user.target.wants/remote-fs.target → /usr/lib/systemd/system/remote-fs.target.
Created symlink /etc/systemd/system/sysinit.target.wants/systemd-pstore.service → /usr/lib/systemd/system/systemd-pstore.service.
Initializing machine ID from random generator.
Assertion 'path_is_absolute(p)' failed at src/basic/chase.c:648, function chase(). Aborting.
Aborted (core dumped)
Assertion 'path_is_absolute(p)' failed at src/basic/chase.c:648, function chase(). Aborting.
Aborted (core dumped)
Assertion 'path_is_absolute(p)' failed at src/basic/chase.c:648, function chase(). Aborting.
Aborted (core dumped)
dpkg: error processing package systemd (--configure):
 installed systemd package post-installation script subprocess returned error exit status 134
Errors were encountered while processing:
 systemd
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)
```

Rolling back to `quay.io/containers/buildah:v1.38.1` fixes the build.
I have the same issue. I confirm that downgrading to buildah v1.38.1 makes it work again.
Does upgrading to 1.39.1 fix it as well?  It's entirely possible that this is the same problem we saw in https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6001.
I can also reproduce using `quay.io/buildah/stable:latest`; downgrading to `stable/v1.38.1` works.

> Does upgrading to 1.39.1 fix it as well? It's entirely possible that this is the same problem we saw in [#6001](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6001).

I'm running into this issue as well. I can't speak for the others, but it doesn't look like there's an image with a `v1.39.1` tag published yet which makes this difficult to test. 😢
The `quay.io/buildah/upstream` image, based off the `main` branch, should also include the change.  Discussion on IRC makes me hopeful.

I don't see an appropriate image for `v1.39.1` in the buildah container registry:

https://quay.io/repository/buildah/stable?tab=tags

So currently I can't test it in the GitLab Pipeline.

I identified what fails in my `Dockerfile` with buildah `v1.39.0`. I'm installing the `postgresql-client-17` from the official repository.

I'm adding the repository with this line in the Dockerfile:

```bash
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/postgresql.gpg] http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt $(lsb_release -cs)-pgdg main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list
```

The `$(lsb_release -cs)` isn't resolved so the `apt update` fails with:

```bash
Err:5 http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt n/a-pgdg Release
  404  Not Found [IP: 147.75.85.69 80]
```

With buildah `v1.38.1` it works and since I'm using `debian:12.9` as a base image the `$(lsb_release -cs)` get's correctly resolved to `bookworm`:

```bash
Get:5 http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt bookworm-pgdg/main amd64 Packages [361 kB]
```

Summarized the command `lsb_release -cs` doesn't work with buildah `v1.39.0` and currently I can't test it with `v1.39.1` since it's missing in the container registry.

I can confirm that with buildah `v1.39.2` the problem is fixed.

Thank you very much!
